scope.summary: Fail closed before a swarm wave spawns when model, quota, context, wave-size, memory, PID/task, or file-descriptor headroom is unsafe.
out_of_scope: Spawning agents itself; the preflight deliberately only returns a decision to the orchestrator.
Provider quota discovery and orchestrator context calculation, which are supplied by the calling harness.

entry_points.name: swarm spawn preflight CLI
entry_points.trace: scripts/swarm_spawn_preflight.sh --selftest passed all 8 deterministic cases: healthy admission plus fail-closed wave ceiling, model mismatch, quota, context, memory, PID/task, and file-descriptor refusals.

entry_points.name: operator wave runbook
entry_points.trace: AGENTS.md and docs/multi-agent-build-policy.md place the preflight before agent launch, document the eight-agent default ceiling and 20 percent context floor, and require the candidate model to match the requested model.

entry_points.name: entry-trace contract
entry_points.trace: scripts/entry_trace_contract.json records scripts/swarm_spawn_preflight.sh as the E3 entry point so repository contract checks cannot silently lose it.

limitations: This close does not claim a required-proof/v1 artifact; readiness remains not-ready on the exact scoped self-test.
Quota and remaining-context values cannot be derived from Linux host state and therefore remain explicit trusted inputs from the orchestrator harness.
